steps.title: Identify the grammatical structures on each side of the blank
steps.content:
Before the blank: “the city of Lüneburg was a member of a powerful mercantile alliance that dominated northern European trade between the 13th and 17th [centuries]” is a complete independent clause.

After the blank: “the Hanseatic League, a loose confederation of cities from eleven modern-day countries that has been described as a precursor to today's European Union” is a noun phrase (with an appositive and relative clause), not an independent clause.

steps.title: Apply the punctuation rule
steps.content:
A colon introduces an explanation or identification following an independent clause.

Here, the Hanseatic League identifies the mercantile alliance just described, so the colon appropriately introduces that identification and elaboration.

Option B (no punctuation) creates a run-on sentence, Option C (period) creates a sentence fragment with no main verb, and Option D (semicolon) violates the rule that a semicolon joins only two independent clauses.

That matches choice A — A is correct.
